title: Job queue vs scheduler vs cron
description: Queues handle event-driven work, schedulers fire time-based work, and naive cron risks overlap and missed runs.
body: Use when choosing how to run background work. A job queue processes work pushed by events with retries and DLQ. A scheduler/cron fires work on a time interval. Do not run cron without overlap protection (a slow run still in progress when the next fires) or without catch-up handling for missed ticks during downtime. Actions: for time-based work add a lock so only one instance runs, make the job idempotent for re-runs, and decide whether missed ticks are skipped or caught up. Validation signals include a test where a long-running job blocks a second trigger and the lock prevents concurrent execution. Risk boundary: overlapping cron runs that double-process data are a correctness defect.
